text: Which of the following describes the difference between a 'Temporary' and a 'Transient' table? (Choose two.)

options.a: Temporary tables have a Fail-safe period; Transient tables do not.
options.b: Transient tables are only visible to the user who created them.
options.c: Permanent tables support up to 90 days of Time Travel (Enterprise); Transient tables are limited to 1 day.
options.d: Transient tables are automatically dropped at the end of the session.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows you to share data with accounts that are in different cloud regions using a 'Direct Share'.
What is the primary function of the 'Information Schema'?

options.a: To store the actual row data for tables.
options.b: To provide a set of views and system functions for querying metadata about objects in a database.
options.c: To manage the credit billing for the account.
options.d: To provide a history of all logins.

text: Which role is recommended for daily administrative tasks such as creating warehouses and managing schemas?
What is the default 'Time Travel' retention period for a new table if not explicitly set?
True or False: In a 'Multi-cluster Warehouse', clusters are always added and removed one at a time.
Which Snowflake component handles the 'Query Optimization' process?
What does the 'STRIP_OUTER_ARRAY' option do when loading JSON data?

options.a: It removes the last character of the JSON string.
options.b: It removes the square brackets at the beginning and end of a JSON file and loads each element as a separate row.

text: True or False: You can set a 'Network Policy' at the Account level and override it at the User level.
Which command is used to see the list of 'Functions' in a schema?
What is the primary benefit of 'Micro-partitions'?

options.a: They allow for data to be stored in an uncompressed format for speed.
options.b: They enable granular pruning, allowing Snowflake to read only the necessary parts of a table.
options.c: They are managed by the user to optimize storage.
options.d: They allow for 'Zero-copy cloning' of individual rows.

text: Which view is used to see the credit consumption for 'Materialized View' maintenance?
True or False: Snowflake 'Shares' can contain objects from multiple different databases.
What is 'Snowpark'?

options.a: A specialized cloud region for Snowflake.
options.b: A developer framework that allows building data applications using non-SQL languages like Python and Java.
options.c: The internal storage engine of Snowflake.
options.d: A training environment for new users.

text: Which command is used to delete all data from a table but keep the table structure?
True or False: All Virtual Warehouses in an account share the same 'Local Disk Cache'.
What is the primary purpose of 'Single Sign-On' (SSO) in Snowflake?

options.a: To allow users to log in with one set of credentials managed by an external identity provider.
options.b: To limit a user to only one active session at a time.

text: Which command is used to upload a file from a local machine to a Snowflake internal stage?
What happens to the 'Time Travel' data when a Permanent table is dropped?

options.b: It is preserved and the table can be recovered using UNDROP within the retention period.
options.d: It is moved to a backup stage.

text: Which role is required to manage 'Organizational' settings and multiple accounts?
True or False: Snowflake 'External Functions' can call a web service hosted on AWS Lambda.
What is the default file format for 'unloading' data if not specified?
Which of the following is true regarding 'Zero-copy cloning'?

options.a: It creates a physical copy of all the data files.
options.b: It creates a new set of metadata pointing to the same data files as the original.
options.c: It is only available for Tables.
options.d: It requires the warehouse to be sized 'Large' or above.

text: What is the purpose of 'Row Access Policies'?

options.a: To limit how many rows can be inserted at once.
options.b: To filter query results based on the user's role and session criteria.
options.c: To speed up the loading of large rows.
options.d: To manage the storage of individual rows.

text: Which Snowflake interface provides a graphical view of a query's execution plan?

options.b: The 'Query Profile' tab in the Web UI
options.c: The 'History' tab in the Web UI

text: Which of the following describes 'Search Optimization' in Snowflake?

options.a: It is a feature that optimizes the search bar in the Snowsight UI.
options.b: It builds a persistent data structure for faster point lookup queries.
options.c: It is only available for semi-structured data types.
options.d: It requires manual re-indexing by the user.

text: True or False: Data in 'Fail-safe' can be queried by users with the ACCOUNTADMIN role.
Which of the following objects can have 'Object Tags' applied? (Choose three.)
What is the primary purpose of 'Single Sign-On' (SSO)?

options.a: To allow users to log into multiple services with one set of credentials.
options.b: To restrict a user to only one active session.
options.c: To provide a second layer of security after the password.

text: Which command is used to see the metadata of all columns in a specific table?
True or False: Snowflake manages the 'scaling up' of a warehouse automatically based on complexity.
Which Snowflake component handles coordination of concurrent queries on a single warehouse?
What happens to credit consumption when a warehouse is 'Suspended'?

options.c: It stops after the 60-second minimum is met.
options.d: It stops at the end of the hour.

text: Which role is the most privileged in the Snowflake RBAC hierarchy?
True or False: Snowflake allows the creation of 'External Tables' in Azure Blob storage.
What is the purpose of 'Time Travel'?

options.b: To access and restore data modified or deleted over a specified window.

text: Which command is used to see the list of 'Resource Monitors'?
True or False: Snowflake charges a premium for optimized storage format compared to raw size.
Which function converts a JSON string into a VARIANT type?
What is the result of 'Scaling Out' a warehouse?

options.b: Adding clusters to handle more concurrent queries.

text: Which role is primarily responsible for managing Users and Roles?
True or False: Snowflake supports 'Multi-factor Authentication' (MFA) for all users.

What is the default character for field delimiting in the COPY command?

options.b: Improves performance of point lookup queries on large tables.

text: What is the benefit of the 'Snowflake Data Marketplace'?

options.b: Discovery and querying of third-party data directly without movement.

text: Which role manages 'Organizational' tasks like creating new accounts?
True or False: Data shared via a 'Share' object is read-only for the consumer.
What is 'Pruning' in Snowflake?

options.b: Skipping micro-partitions that don't contain data relevant to the query based on metadata.

text: Which edition is the first to include 'Time Travel' up to 90 days?

options.b: Stops load and rolls back transaction if any error is encountered.

options.b: Obscuring sensitive results based on user role without changing data.
